reviews.name: Hannah D.
reviews.text: We celebrated my husband’s birthday at The Graceful Ordinary and it was such a great evening. From the moment we sat down, the service was fantastic, very attentive, knowledgeable, and genuinely warm without ever feeling overbearing. The food was incredible. The venison was cooked perfectly and packed with flavor, the Humboldt Fog goat cheese was rich and creamy in the best way, and the sea bass was outstandinglight, flaky, and beautifully balanced. Every dish felt thoughtful and elevated. But the real standout? The cocktails. The TGO Clover and Cabin Fever were next-level. Creative, perfectly balanced, and honestly some of the best cocktails we’ve had in a long time. If you go, do not skip the drinks—they’re worth the visit alone. The vibe was lively and energetic, which we loved for a birthday celebration. It does get a bit noisy, so just something for others to be aware of, but it absolutely adds to the bustling, fun atmosphere. Overall, amazing food, killer cocktails, great service, and a vibrant scene. We had a wonderful time and will definitely be back. 🍸✨

reviews.name: Kari Christensen, ABR, REALTOR
reviews.text: We had a wonderful experience at Graceful Ordinary, one that's hard to match outside of Chicago. From its comfortable yet elegant design to the unique menu, every detail was carefully considered. The food was delicious and thoughtfully presented, and the service was excellent. We were fortunate to visit on a very warm day towards the end of September. We sat on the fully screened-in veranda and enjoyed the scenery in comfort. We highly recommend this restaurant to anyone seeking a unique, elevated dining experience; no matter where you're traveling from, it's worth the trip.

reviews.name: Greg Maslowski
reviews.text: Cozy, dark, intament vibe. Very experienced bartenders, servers, runners, chefs. You are treated like family there. Skip downtown Chicago and come out here for a upper excelon experience in the suburbs. Reservations matter unless you can snag a seat at the bar, but bar is small. Plan to spend at least $120 per person. If you can live with that, you won't be disappointed. If you can get Tiffany as your server, you are in luck. She's very knowledgeable, a superior server and just an easy to talk to person that makes your experience that much better.🍻

reviews.name: Bree
reviews.text: This place has cool vibes, friendly service and delicious food. It is pricey but worth it. We had the pierogis which were delicious, the monkfish and venison were also perfectly prepared. The fig and the furious cocktail was 10/10. The old fashioned was fine.

reviews.name: Karla Wagner
reviews.text: Tiffany Cozzi made our dining experience delightful and fun! And Miles is an amazing bar tender who shares the unique ingredients that elevates their cocktails. Try the espresso martini!

reviews.name: Kalee Lee
reviews.text: Thank you to the Graceful Ordinary team for creating a beautiful experience for our micro wedding and upon our return this past weekend for our one year anniversary. The food is truly an experience. The care and detail put into the service is unforgettable. We will always recommend anyone visiting St. Charles stop there for nothing but quality and excellence!

reviews.name: Mike Vaiana
reviews.text: I visited this establishment for Valentine's Day and enjoyed the 8oz Denver Wagyu and my significant other had the Venison, recommended to temp by our waitress. First of all, walking in felt like taking a portal into a much calmer and elegant life. The hostesses were kind and attentive, they even took our coats! The whole place is beautiful, and extremely unique. Once we had our seats it was obvious we were in for a treat. You can see the staff including the cooks from a view into the kitchen and the impressive wood fire oven and stove. The cooks all appeared so calm and collected and I've observed them clearly in their element. Shout out to the homie with the headband, dude was COOKIN. Get him a GoPro and a YouTube channel. Shannon was our waitress and she was extremely professional, offered advice and really catered to us having a great experience on Valentine's day. The Steak and Venison were incredible, perfectly cooked. I had mine medium rare and hers was medium. Peanut butter chocolate cake and Banana Ice cream desert hit the spot just before we left. Highly recommended.

reviews.name: Marc-Louis Paprzyca
reviews.text: My wife took me for my birthday dinner after noticing Chef Curren’s name. He was the chef at one of our favorite restaurants in Chicago years ago, Blue 13. The Graceful Ordinary did not disappoint. Inventive drinks and dishes with beautiful colors and flavors. The bread to start was amazing. Then we had the Maytag Whipped Bleu Cheese and Sweet Corn Ravioli which were wonderful. I really enjoyed the Brussel Sprouts and the Sweet Corn Berry Sundae. They took some of my favorite flavors, which I am very discerning about, and elevated them. We sat on the veranda which was lovely on a Friday night in August but I loved the look of the inside. I can’t wait to go back again and have more dishes.

reviews.name: neil pleskovitch
reviews.text: We had the most wonderful Sunday brunch! There was a little confusion in regards to our reservation, but the manager handled it with absolute grace and made for a perfect afternoon celebrating a friend. The lobster biscuits and gravy are not to be missed, and the beignets were next level. Very creative cocktail menu hits all the right notes as well. Our server was absolutely delightful and made our experience that much more wonderful. Stylish thoughtfully designed rooms with a view of the Fox River makes this a destination. Highly recommend!

reviews.name: K
reviews.text: Went here with my sister for the first time tonight. The service, ambience, and food were fantastic. Our server was very attentive. We started off with the poached pear and deviled eggs snacks which were both delicious. I got the sea bass and my sister got the vegetable curry for our entrees . Both were amazing. The sea bass was perfectly cooked and everything was seasoned well. Will definitely be back.

reviews.name: Mark Kukla
reviews.text: Wow! Best Restaurant in the burbs by far! I have been wanting to dine here for a while now and finally had a date night with my wife for her birthday. 10++, this restaurant was amazing on all fronts. The hostess was super friendly and warm welcoming. The atmosphere was comforting and elegant hip. We waited for our table near the bar and the bartender stopped and asked if we would like to order a drink. (That never happens elsewhere). We were taken to our table which was facing their open kitchen where we watched the magic of the chefs take place. It was impressive to watch the chefs work together in perfect harmony. Seeing the fire and smelling the wood burning was relaxing and exciting. Our server was 10++, always allowing time between meals, caring, knowledgeable, and friendly. Real burning candles on every table made for a special feeling. The food was out of this world! It was the perfect meal. They had non alcoholic options, appetizers were divine, and the meal was everything I wanted!!! Dessert was out of this world. It was a very special dining experience and I loved every minute of it. Ever staff member was happy and friendly. Such a wonderful place. I’m so glad I was able to visit. I will be back.

reviews.name: Jerry Gendron
reviews.text: Megan, Our family gathering has been much anticipated for several weeks, and we weren't at all disappointed. The food was excellent; the floral arrangements which I left to your discretion was beautiful and well received. And of course our requested server, Shannon, is absolutely the best! We couldn't ask for better. Your venue is our favorite in St. Charles; loved the private dinning room! We look forward to our next visit.

reviews.name: Erica Thomas
reviews.text: I’ve been here several times now, for brunch, lunch, and dinner, and truly enjoy this spot. Great cocktails and so many fun dishes to try, I like going back every few months to see the new seasonal options. The cinnamon roll was a favorite of our brunch group, plus their rotating pastry selection is a must-try. Their oysters are pretty fresh, which is not easy to find. For dinner, we loved the bread service and the pastas. I always look forward to going here!

reviews.name: Vladyslav Terletskyy
reviews.text: I had an incredible dining experience during my recent visit! The atmosphere was warm and welcoming, the food was excellent, and the entire evening was made even better thanks to our server, Tiffany. Tiffany was absolutely outstanding—attentive without being intrusive, genuinely friendly, and incredibly knowledgeable about the menu. She offered great recommendations, checked in at just the right moments, and made sure every detail of our meal was perfect. It’s rare to find service that feels both professional and personal, and Tiffany absolutely delivered. The quality of the dishes matched the level of service: everything was fresh, beautifully presented, and full of flavor. From start to finish, the experience was seamless. Highly recommend dining here—and if you’re lucky enough to have Tiffany as your server, you’re in for an exceptional meal and an even better experience!

reviews.name: Natalya Rappoport
reviews.text: Came here for dinner with good friends. Had an amazing time. Great food and drinks. Beautiful view of the water off the terrace, and it was nice with fire pits. We had bone marrow appetizer, and I could just eat that with bread . Sea bass was good, steak was very delicious. And service was outstanding!! Worth the experience!

reviews.name: Feruza Bonu
reviews.text: A wonderful dining experience overall — the atmosphere, service, and flavors were excellent. The scallops were perfectly cooked and very enjoyable, though I was a little disappointed to see that the potato side dish was removed. Nevertheless, everything else was outstanding

reviews.name: Ronald Diamond
reviews.text: We were told hour wait for a table so took an immediate seat at the bar. Excellent choice! Tried a few different craft beers and they were Excellent. Wild game burger and brie apple and bacon flatbread both were fabulous!

reviews.name: Emily Frey
reviews.text: Wonderful experience! Excellent food, beautiful atmosphere, and Tiffany was fabulous! She had excellent timing, a great attitude, and and lovely service. We had the pimento biscuits, the lox and latkes, and the brunch waffles. The en fuego cocktail was delicious, so was the bloody Mary. Happy to go back again!!!

reviews.name: Curtis Nelson
reviews.text: wish I could give this place 6 stars! I love the ambiance of the place. I’ve only had the opportunity to eat inside but am looking forward to trying the outside deck with views of the river when it gets warmer! The service is amazing. Even though it’s packed, you’re never sitting around waiting and if you ask for recommendations, they go above and beyond! and the food? I’ve tried the Venison and the Sea Bass and both were cooked perfectly and so tasty! I’ll definitely be back!!!!

reviews.name: Courtney Dixon
reviews.text: Stunning! I visited for a high tea event and it was incredible! Food was spectacular and the ambiance was beyond comparison! Highly recommend!

reviews.name: Nicholas Knaperek
reviews.text: Absolutely incredible. Across the board. Dan, our extremely knowledgeable waiter, took the lead and guided us through an unforgettable tasting/pairing menu. Claire, the bartender with an amazing taste, built us cocktails that paired perfectly with the meals. Finally Chris, who created such an incredible world for us to be apart of, we cannot thank you enough. This is a must visit place. Something to take you out of the everyday.

reviews.name: Katy G
reviews.text: The Graceful Ordinary has become one of our favorite spots. The atmosphere alone is worth the visit — the candlelit tables and the holiday decorations this time of year make the whole place feel warm, cozy, and a little magical. I’ve never had a single thing here I didn’t love. Everything always comes out beautifully prepared and delicious. If you’re looking for a great night out, the Graceful Ordinary never disappoints! Also, shout out to our favorite server, Tiffany, who is so kind, personal, and attentive 💕

reviews.name: Kimberly G
reviews.text: Had a wonderful brunch here! The food was all fantastic. Huge cocktail menu including some interesting N/A options. The service was excellent. Overall, this is a great place for a special occasion meal.

reviews.name: Avery Davies
reviews.text: The service here from beginning to end was spectacular. Shannon, our server, was energetic, knowledgeable, and attentive. The food was exquisite. We enjoyed a grilled salad... who would of thought you could grill lettuce over an open fire to perfection! The rib eye was as tender as a filet and so very flavorful. A scrumptious side of brussel sprouts topped off our meal. The finale was the sweet potato ice cream sundae. So incredibly delicious. If you like the fall flavors of pumpkin spices this is not to miss. Thank you and kudos to the staff at the Graceful Ordinary for a wonderful night out with my husband.

reviews.name: Megan Walsen
reviews.text: We loved our experience at The Graceful Ordinary! We had our very intimate wedding ceremony and reception in the private dining room. Our food, drinks, and service were all fantastic. Working with Megan, the event coordinator, was easy and she was really helpful along the way. We’re excited to go back to The Graceful Ordinary for a night out and for future special events. Highly recommend!

reviews.name: Al Hung
reviews.text: My wife and I celebrated her birthday at The Graceful Ordinary this past weekend, and it turned out to be one of the best birthday dinners either of us has ever had. From the service to the quality of food to the atmosphere, nearly everything was perfect. 1. Service: we were being taken care of by Katy if I remember correctly. We arrived for dinner at 5:30 on a Saturday so things were busy, and Katy was super attentive and we never felt like we had to wait for anything. We brought our own cake, which the kitchen very gracefully allowed us to substitute in lieu of their own deserts, which was cut and served with coffee after the meal. There was a snafu with the steak my wife ordered, and instead of asking us to wait for the proper order to come out, we were invited to dig into the "wrong" order (which was still a delicious petite filet) while the kitchen prepared the right steak. The on-site somalier also provided a great little review and history of the bottle of pinot noir we ordered. 2. Quality of food: I don't remember the exact names of the dishes we ordered so I'm simplifying, but trust me they were all delicious. An octopus appetizer, a roasted shishito pepper side, a roasted cabbage side, and then a ribeye for the main along with the petite filet mignon, both done to medium rare. The flavors were interesting without being outlandish, especially the octopus and shishito peppers side. I trust there's nothing bad on this menu. 3. Atmosphere: It's a beautiful setting and place to enjoy a meal. During warmer weather the balcony seats along the river must be particularly enjoyable but we chose to sit in the main dining room, which felt spacious. We were at a back corner table, and didn't have any trouble having a conversation at all. We'll absolutely be back. The proximity to Pollyana's brewpub is a huge plus too. Thank you Graceful Ordinary team for a fantastic and memorable dinner.

reviews.name: Lauren Breeden
reviews.text: This has become a fave date night spot! Start with the Saint 75 cocktail and the marinated olives and the bread cheese--so much flavor. We love the mussels and pasta. The oysters and shrimp and grits are wonderful too. On our third visit we finally ordered dessert and the butterscotch pot de creme was fantastic. 😍Beautiful ambiance too. …

reviews.name: Linda Franzen
reviews.text: The Graceful Ordinary has a gorgeous bar and dining room that is located in a quaint area of St. Charles along the river. The kitchen area is open to the dining room so you can watch them preparing food if you are seated in that section of the dining room. We started with the Maytag Bleu Cheese whipped with poached pear and pistachio granola. It was excellent and was served with bread. Next we had the Waldorf Salad with smoked grapes, pickled apples and vinaigrette, a very unique twist on the traditional Waldorf Salad. For our entrees we both had the Cajun Catfish, it was outstanding! Service was attentive and the restaurant is quite loud, but there is seating available outside. A creative addition to the St. Charles and Geneva restaurant scene. We will definitely return.

reviews.name: John Schutze
reviews.text: AMAZING! From the gorgeous view of the Fox River to the modern-yet-classy decor, this place manages to be both impressive and inviting. Of course, the star of the show is the top-notch food that comes from the open kitchen area. We dined here on NYE, and rarely have I had such well prepared lobster or scallops. Creative flavor combinations in the buffalo cauliflower elevated what I expected to be a pub-dish taste to something magnificent. In addition to a well selected drink pairing list, they had amazing cocktails and some great local beers on tap, too! Can't recommend this place enough. Note: Prior review said staff unmasked, but staff was fully masked when we were there.

reviews.name: Mindy Wilmington
reviews.text: Went there to celebrate several birthdays. Drinks were good. All of the dishes were great, especially the blue crab croquettes, scallops, and brussel sprouts. What put the night over the top was our server, Tiffany! We will go back for that alone.

reviews.name: David Swiston
reviews.text: Wife and I ate here for my birthday. All aspects were great. There were a variety of creative food options. The staff was attentive. Nice place for a fancy special occasion.

reviews.name: saranya bhaskar
reviews.text: Loved the Valentine’s Day menu and the food tasted so good!!

reviews.name: Shawn Williams
reviews.text: We just had the pleasure of using the private party room for 14 of our immediate family members for my wife’s 30th birthday and our 2nd baby’s gender reveal. Let me start off by saying the management (Megan Curren & her team) are absolutely wonderful. We had something come up last minute and Megan quickly worked with me to come up with a resolution to mediate the situation. Not only did she offer to reschedule for us but if we couldn’t then she was going to bring the price down. That is what you call AMAZING customer service. Now onto the food, drinks, atmosphere and servers. I can not honestly say one bad thing about any aspect. Our server was better than most servers I have a regular sit down restaurants, but to be able to handle 14 of my a bit crazy family is definitely an accomplishment. Now as I’m sure you’ve been waiting let’s get right into the food and drinks. If you enjoy creative, new ideas, great flavor and execution this is the place. From the salad to the pork chop to the whole fish, everything was SPOT ON! Compliments to The chefs and his team on flawless taste and presentation of each and every plate. Compliments to the bar tender and the staff because my second drink was just as good as the first and so on. If you want a new creative food option, ascetically pleasing exterior and interior and staff that doesn’t miss a beat, this is your spot! Thank you Megan and the rest of the team for an outstanding celebration last night! We look forward to coming back to enjoy a few more plates which I’m sure amazing!

reviews.name: Beth Howley
reviews.text: We hosted a wedding shower on April 2nd for my son and his fiancé. Everything was incredible! The food, the staff and the atmosphere. Everyone was blown away. I highly recommend The Graceful Ordinary for an incredible experience! Megan and Chris' team are the best!

reviews.name: Bailey Burke
reviews.text: The MOST amazing restaurant ! The food is beyond incredible, the drinks are beautiful, the dessert…OH MY GOD! the ambiance and vibe 10/10. Well worth it for a wonderful night out. We enjoyed every bit of our evening here.
